His saying, the Exalted: **وَهُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ كُلٌّ فِي فَلَكٍ يَسْبَحُونَ** "It is He who created the night and the day, the sun and the moon, each swimming in an orbit" (21:33). The verse is apparent in establishing an orbit (الفلك) for each of the following: the night—which is the conical shadow attached to the face of the earth opposite to the sun; the day—which is the opposite of the night; and the sun and the moon. Thus, what is meant by "orbit" (الفلك) is the path of each of them.

Furthermore, what is intended is the explanation of the positions and states occurring relative to the earth and in its atmosphere, even if the state of other celestial bodies is different. For there is no night or day corresponding to it for the sun and other fixed stars, which are luminous by essence; and for the moon and other planets that acquire light, their night and day are different from ours.

His saying: **يَسْبَحُونَ** "swimming" is from *al-sabh* (السبح), meaning moving through water by cleaving it. It is said that He used the form **يَسْبَحُونَ** (the plural for rational beings) because He attributed to them the action of rational beings, as He said: **وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ رَأَيْتُهُمْ لِي ساجِدِينَ** "and the sun and the moon: I saw them prostrating to me" (12:4).

## (Commentary)

In *al-Mahasin*, with its chain of transmission from Yunus in a *marfu’* report, he said: Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) said: **ليس من باطل يقوم بإزاء حق ـ إلا غلب الحق الباطل وذلك قول الله : « بَلْ نَقْذِفُ بِالْحَقِّ عَلَى الْباطِلِ فَيَدْمَغُهُ فَإِذا هُوَ زاهِقٌ »** — "There is no falsehood that stands against a truth except that the truth overcomes the falsehood, and that is the saying of Allah: 'Rather We hurl the truth against falsehood, and it crushes its head, and behold, it vanishes.'"

In the same source, with its chain of transmission from Ayyub ibn al-Hurr, he said: Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) said: **يا أيوب ما من أحد إلا وقد يرد عليه الحق ـ حتى يصدع قلبه قبله أم تركه ـ وذلك أن الله يقول في كتابه : « بَلْ نَقْذِفُ بِالْحَقِّ عَلَى الْباطِلِ ـ فَيَدْمَغُهُ فَإِذا هُوَ زاهِقٌ وَلَكُمُ الْوَيْلُ مِمَّا تَصِفُونَ »** — "O Ayyub, there is no one but that the truth is presented to him until it cleaves his heart—whether he accepts it or leaves it—and that is because Allah says in His Book: 'Rather We hurl the truth against falsehood, and it crushes its head, and behold, it vanishes. And woe to you for what you allege.'"

**I say:** The two narrations are based on the generalization of the verse.

In *al-Uyun*, in the chapter on what has been narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) regarding Harut and Marut, in a hadith: "That the angels are infallible (معصومون)—protected from disbelief and foul deeds by the graces of Allah, the Exalted." Allah, the Exalted, said regarding them: **لا يَعْصُونَ اللهَ ما أَمَرَهُمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ ما يُؤْمَرُونَ** — "they do not disobey Allah in what He commands them, and they carry out what they are commanded" (66:6). And the Mighty and Majestic said: **وَلَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَنْ عِنْدَهُ** — meaning the angels — **لا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبادَتِهِ وَلا يَسْتَحْسِرُونَ يُسَبِّحُونَ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ لا يَفْتُرُونَ** — "To Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth, and those who are with Him are not too proud to worship Him, nor do they weary. They celebrate His glory night and day, and they do not flag" (21:19-20).

In *Nahj al-Balaghah*, he (peace be upon him) said in describing the angels: **ومسبحون لا يسأمون ، ولا يغشاهم نوم العيون ، ولا سهو العقول ، ولا فترة الأبدان ، ولا غفلة النسيان** — "And they are glorifiers who do not tire; they are not overcome by the sleep of eyes, nor the oversight of intellects, nor the flagging of bodies, nor the heedlessness of forgetfulness."

**I say:** By this, what is found in some narrations that the angels sleep is weakened, as in the book *Kamal al-Din*, with its chain of transmission from Dawud ibn Farqad from some of our companions from Abu ‘Abdillah: that he was asked about the angels, do they sleep? He said: **ما من حي إلا وهو ينام خلا الله وحده** — "There is no living thing but that it sleeps, except Allah alone." I said: "Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: 'They celebrate His glory night and day, and they do not flag'?" He said: **أنفاسهم تسبيح** — "Their breaths are glorification." Furthermore, the narration is weak.

In *al-Tawhid*, with its chain of transmission from Hisham ibn al-Hakam, he said: I said to Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him): "What is the proof that Allah is One?" He said: **اتصال التدبير وتمام الصنع كما قال عز وجل : « لَوْ كانَ فِيهِما آلِهَةٌ إِلَّا اللهُ لَفَسَدَتا »** — "The continuity of governance and the perfection of craftsmanship, as the Mighty and Majestic said: 'Had there been any gods in them besides Allah, they would surely have fallen apart.'"

**I say:** This supports what we previously mentioned in the presentation of the proof.

In the same source, with its chain of transmission from ‘Amr ibn Jabir, he said: I said to Abu Ja'far Muhammad ibn ‘Ali al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "O son of the Messenger of Allah, we see children, some of whom are born dead, some are miscarried incomplete, some are born blind, mute, or deaf, some die the moment they fall to the ground, some remain until puberty, and some live until they become old men. How is that, and what is the reason for it?"

He (peace be upon him) said: **إن الله تبارك وتعالى ـ أولى بما يدبره من أمر خلقه منهم ـ وهو الخالق والمالك لهم فمن منعه التعمير ـ فإنما منعه ما ليس له ، ومن عمره فإنما أعطاه ما ليس له ـ فهو المتفضل بما أعطى ـ وعادل فيما منع ولا يسأل عما يفعل وهم يسألون** — "Indeed Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, is more entitled to govern the affairs of His creation than they are. He is the Creator and the Owner of them. So whoever He denies long life, He has only denied him what was not his; and whoever He grants long life, He has only given him what was not his. Thus, He is the Bestower of grace in what He gives, and Just in what He withholds; 'He is not questioned about what He does, but they are questioned.'"

Jabir said: I said to him: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, and how is He not questioned about what He does?" He said: **لأنه لا يفعل إلا ما كان حكمة وصوابا ، وهو المتكبر الجبار والواحد القهار ، فمن وجد في نفسه حرجا في شيء مما قضى كفر ـ ومن أنكر شيئا من أفعاله جحد** — "Because He does not do anything except that it is wisdom and correctness. He is the All-Magnanimous, the All-Compeller, the One, the All-Subduer. So whoever finds in himself any grievance regarding anything He has decreed has disbelieved, and whoever denies any of His acts has rejected [the truth]."

**I say:** This is a noble narration that provides a universal principle (أصلا كليا) regarding good and evil: that good things are existential matters (أمور وجودية) that trace back to His giving and grace, the Exalted, while evil things are privative matters (أمور عدمية) that end in the non-giving of what the servant does not own. As for his (peace be upon him) saying that He, the Exalted, is more entitled to what belongs to His servant than the servant himself, its reason is that He, the Exalted, is the Owner of the servant's essence, and the servant only owns what he owns through the ownership granted by Him, the Exalted; thus He is the Owner of what He has made the servant own, and the servant's ownership is longitudinal (في طول) to His ownership.

His saying: "Because He does not do anything except that it is wisdom and correctness" is an allusion to the first presentation we provided. His saying: "He is the All-Magnanimous, the All-Compeller, the One, the All-Subduer" is an allusion to the second presentation we mentioned in the commentary of the verse.

In *Nur al-Thaqalayn*, from al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, said: **يا بن آدم بمشيتي كنت أنت الذي تشاء لنفسك ما تشاء ، وبقوتي أديت إلي فرائضي ، وبنعمتي قويت على معصيتي ـ جعلتك سميعا بصيرا قويا ـ ما أصابك من حسنة فمن الله ـ وما أصابك من سيئة فمن نفسك ـ وذلك أني أولى بحسناتك منك ـ وأنت أولى بسيئاتك مني ـ وذلك أني لا أسأل عما أفعل وهم يسألون** — "O son of Adam, by My will you were the one who wills for yourself what you will; by My power you performed My obligations to Me; and by My blessing you were strong enough to disobey Me. I made you hearing, seeing, and strong. Whatever good befalls you is from Allah, and whatever evil befalls you is from yourself. That is because I am more entitled to your good deeds than you are, and you are more entitled to your evil deeds than I am. That is because I am not questioned about what I do, but they are questioned."

In *al-Majma'*, regarding His saying, the Exalted: **هذا ذِكْرُ مَنْ مَعِيَ وَذِكْرُ مَنْ قَبْلِي** — "This is a reminder of those who are with me and a reminder of those before me" (21:24)—Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) said: **يعني بذكر من معي ما هو كائن ـ وبذكر من قبلي ما قد كان** — "He means by the reminder of those with me what is to be, and by the reminder of those before me what has been."

In *al-Uyun*, with its chain of transmission to al-Husayn ibn Khalid from ‘Ali ibn Musa al-Rida from his father from his forefathers from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny) said: **من لم يؤمن بحوضي فلا أورده الله حوضي ، ومن لم يؤمن بشفاعتي فلا أناله الله شفاعتي ، ثم قال عليه‌السلام : إنما شفاعتي لأهل الكبائر من أمتي ـ فأما المحسنون فما عليهم من سبيل** — "Whoever does not believe in my Pond (الحوض), may Allah not bring him to my Pond; and whoever does not believe in my intercession, may Allah not grant him my intercession." Then he (peace be upon him) said: "My intercession is only for the people of major sins from my community; as for the virtuous, there is no way against them."

Al-Husayn ibn Khalid said: I said to al-Rida (peace be upon him): "O son of the Messenger of Allah, then what is the meaning of the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: 'and they do not intercede except for someone He approves of'?" He said: **لا يشفعون إلا لمن ارتضى الله دينه** — "They do not intercede except for someone whose religion Allah approves of."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, al-Hakim narrated and authenticated it, as did al-Bayhaqi in *al-Ba'th*, from Jabir: that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny) recited the saying of Allah: **وَلا يَشْفَعُونَ إِلَّا لِمَنِ ارْتَضى** and then said: **إن شفاعتي لأهل الكبائر من أمتي** — "Indeed, my intercession is for the people of major sins from my community."

In *al-Ihtijaj*, it is narrated: that ‘Amr ibn ‘Ubayd came to Muhammad ibn ‘Ali al-Baqir (peace be upon him) to test him with questions. He said to him: "May I be your ransom, what is the meaning of His saying, the Exalted: 'Have the faithless not regarded that the heavens and the earth were a solid mass and We clove them asunder'—what is this solid mass (الرتق) and claving asunder (الفتق)?" Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) said: **كانت السماء رتقا لا تنزل القطر ـ وكانت الأرض رتقا لا تخرج النبات ـ ففتق الله السماء بالقطر وفتق الأرض بالنبات** — "The sky was a solid mass that did not send down rain, and the earth was a solid mass that did not bring forth vegetation; so Allah clove the sky with rain and clove the earth with vegetation." ‘Amr ibn ‘Ubayd was silenced, found no objection, and left.

**I say:** This meaning is narrated in *Rawdat al-Kafi* from him (peace be upon him) through two chains.

In *Nahj al-Balaghah*, he (peace be upon him) said: **وفتق بعد الارتتاق صوامت أبوابها** — "And He clove, after the state of being a solid mass, the silent ones of its doors."
